name: Viachaslau Pratasenia
subtitle: I build apps with Flutter and lead a small team that ships them.

about_me.title: Flutter developer and Mobile Team Lead, 7+ years in the industry.
about_me.text:
I started out writing Android apps, and that background still shapes how I work — I care about apps that feel fast, make sense, and don't get in the user's way. These days I build with Flutter across Android, iOS, web, and desktop from one codebase.

Now I lead a mobile team. A lot of that job is the unglamorous part: planning, one-on-ones, code reviews, keeping people unblocked. I also write code every day, so I stay close to the problems the team actually hits.

AI has become a normal part of how we work. I use AI assistants daily to move faster on the boring parts of development, and I build automation with n8n and AI agents to take repetitive work off our plates. On the quality side, I set up end-to-end testing with Patrol so our Flutter apps get tested the way a real user would tap through them — not just in isolated unit tests.

At Aventus IT I focus on shipping apps that are genuinely easy to use, and on keeping the team in good shape to keep shipping them.

contact_me.title: Not actively looking, but the inbox is always open.
contact_me.text: Got a question, an idea, or just want to say hi? Drop me a line — I read everything and try to reply.

work.title: Flutter Developer / Mobile Team Lead
work.description:
I lead a three-person mobile team on an e-commerce project and also develop 7VPN, shipping across iOS, macOS, Windows, and Web.

The lead part of the job: technical calls, one-on-ones, standups, retros, planning, and docs. On the build side I shipped the personal account website, social login, and the news feature, plus deep links, push notifications, and the build environments the team relies on.

Lately I've leaned into two things. First, end-to-end testing with Patrol, so our Flutter apps are tested through real user flows across platforms instead of unit tests alone. Second, AI in our workflow — AI assistants for day-to-day development and n8n with AI agents to automate the repetitive work around the team.

Tools: Xcode, Android Studio, Figma, Firebase, Google Cloud Platform, Patrol, n8n.

work.title: Flutter Developer
work.description:
At Symfa I worked on two products: a dating app and a corporate chat in the spirit of Slack. The work spanned a lot of ground — wiring up Redux, moving the codebase to null safety, and keeping up with new Flutter releases.

I also handled the publishing side end to end. When Google Play or the App Store flagged something, I was the one going back and forth with their review teams to sort it out and get users a fix.

Tools: Xcode, Android Studio, Figma, Firebase.

work.title: Android/Flutter Developer
work.description:
At Heapix I worked as an Android/Flutter developer across a pretty mixed bag of projects — food delivery, smart home control, a few others. Several of them I started from an empty repo.

Working closely with design and management taught me as much as the code did: how to talk to clients, scope things realistically, and pick up the project-management side along the way.

Tools: Android Studio, Figma, Firebase, Adobe Illustrator.

projects.title: Personal Website
projects.description: This site, built with Flutter and hosted on GitHub Pages. I kept it clean and simple on purpose. The source is public, so you can dig into how it's put together — and it's a decent example of Flutter holding its own on the web.

projects.title: Never Have I Ever
projects.description: A mobile take on the party game — 10+ categories and 2000+ questions to play through with friends. Built with Flutter and published on Google Play.

projects.title: Workout Tracker App Design
projects.description: A workout tracker concept I designed in Figma. The whole idea was to keep it simple — log a workout fast, glance at your progress, get out. Clean look, no clutter.

blog_posts.title: The Easiest Way to Use Environments in Flutter
blog_posts.description: How to handle environment variables in Flutter without pulling in extra libraries — keeping config out of your code and easy to change.

blog_posts.title: Styling Flutter Apps Made Easy with Google Fonts
blog_posts.description: A quick walkthrough of using Google Fonts in Flutter — picking and swapping fonts with almost no effort to give your app a bit more character.

blog_posts.title: Flutter Version Management: A Comprehensive Guide
blog_posts.description: A practical guide to FVM for juggling multiple Flutter SDKs — installing versions, switching between them, and keeping projects consistent.
